blocks:
Sūrat al-Infiṭār

Meccan, and it is 19 verses.

Sūrat Infaṭarat

And it is called Sūrat al-Infiṭār, Meccan, its number of verses is nineteen verses without dispute (بلا خلاف).* بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ*** (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١)إِذَا السَّماءُ انْفَطَرَتْ (When the sky is split open) It is split open/cracked. (٢)وَإِذَا الْكَواكِبُ انْتَثَرَتْ (and when the stars are scattered,) They fall down, scattered. (٣)وَإِذَا الْبِحارُ فُجِّرَتْ (and when the seas are merged,) Some of them are opened up to others, so the entirety becomes one sea. (٤)وَإِذَا الْقُبُورُ بُعْثِرَتْ (and when the graves are overturned,) Its dust is overturned and its dead are brought forth. It is said that it is composed of (بعث) and (وراء الاثارة). Al-Qummi said: The earth splits open and the people emerge from it. (٥)عَلِمَتْ نَفْسٌ ما قَدَّمَتْ وَأَخَّرَتْ (every soul will know what it has sent ahead and what it has left behind.) Meaning, of good and evil. And it is said: And what it left behind of a good practice (سنّة حسنة) that was followed after it, or a bad practice (سنّة سيّئة) that was followed after it. And this is the answer to (إذا) [in the preceding verses]. (٦)يا أَيُّهَا الْإِنْسانُ ما غَرَّكَ بِرَبِّكَ الْكَرِيمِ (O man! What has deceived you concerning your generous Lord,) What thing has deceived you and emboldened you to disobey Him? It is said that the mention of الْكَرِيمِ (the Generous) is for emphasis in preventing deception and to indicate that by which Satan deceives him, for Satan says to him: 'Do whatever you wish, for your Lord is Generous and will not punish anyone.' And it is said that the Glorified One only said الْكَرِيمِ (the Generous) and not His other Names and Attributes because it is as if He taught him the answer, so that he might say: 'The Generous One's generosity (كريم الكريم) deceived me.' In al-Majma', it is narrated: When the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم recited this verse, He said: "His ignorance (جهله) deceived him."

(7) الَّذِي خَلَقَكَ فَسَوَّاكَ He made your limbs sound, proportioned, and prepared for their benefits. فَعَدَلَكَ He made your structure moderate, with proportionate limbs. And it was read (وقرئ) with takhfif (التخفيف), meaning He made some of your limbs equal (عدّل) to others until they became balanced (اعتدلت). (7) Who created you and proportioned you, (8) فِي أَيِّ صُورَةٍ ما شاءَ رَكَّبَكَ Meaning, He composed you in whatever form He wished, and mā (ما) is extra (مزيدة). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (عليه السلام) and al-Qummi: They said: Had He wished, He would have composed you in a form other than this form. (8) and composed you in whatever form He wished? (9) كَلَّا A deterrent against being deluded by the generosity of Allah. بَلْ تُكَذِّبُونَ بِالدِّينِ A transition (اضراب) to what is the original cause of the delusion, and al-Dīn (الدّين) is the recompense (الجزاء) or Islam. Al-Qummi said: [It refers] to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم and the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. (9) No indeed! Rather you deny the Retribution.

(١٠) وَإِنَّ عَلَيْكُمْ لَحافِظِينَ ** (10) Indeed there are guardians over you, Said the two angels entrusted with the human being. (١١) كِراماً كاتِبِينَ ** (11) noble scribes, They hasten to write the good deeds for you and they delay writing the evil deeds against you, so that perhaps you may repent and seek forgiveness. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: "When the servant intends a good deed (الحسنة), his breath comes out with a pleasant scent. The angel on the right says to the angel on the left: 'Stop, for he has intended a good deed.' Then, when he performs it, his tongue is his pen and his saliva is his ink, so he records it for him. And when he intends an evil deed (السيّئة), his breath comes out with a foul odor. The angel on the left says to the angel on the right: 'Stop, for he has intended an evil deed.' Then, when he commits it, his saliva is his ink and his tongue is his pen, so he records it against him." It was said that they were only named كِراماً ** (noble) because when they write a good deed (حسنة), they ascend with it to the heaven and present it to Allah, the Exalted, and bear witness to that. They say: "Indeed, Your servant so-and-so performed such-and-such a good deed." And when they write an evil deed (سيّئة) from the servant, they ascend with it to the heaven with grief and sorrow. Allah, the Exalted, says: "What has My servant done?" So they remain silent until Allah asks a second and a third time. Then they say: "O my God (إلهي), You are the Concealer (ستّار), and You commanded Your servants to conceal their faults; conceal their faults. And You are the Knower of the Unseen (علّام الغيوب)." And for this reason, they are named كِراماً كاتِبِينَ.** (noble scribes).

(١٢) يَعْلَمُونَ ما تَفْعَلُونَ. (12) They know what you do. In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): that He (peace be upon him) was asked: "What is the reason (علّة) for the two angels assigned (الموكّلين) to His servants, who write down what is against them and what is for them, while Allah is the Knower of the secret (السرّ) and what is even more hidden (أخفى)?" He said: "He made them servants (استعبدهم) through that, and made them witnesses (شهوداً) over His creation, so that the servants, due to the angels' constant presence (ملازمتهم) with them, would be more diligent (أشدّ مواظبة) in obeying Allah and more restrained (أشدّ انقباضاً) from disobeying Him. And how many a servant intends (يهمّ) a sin, then remembers their place (the angels' presence), so he repents (فارعوى) and refrains (وكفّ), saying: 'My Lord has seen me, and my guardians (حفظتي) will bear witness (تشهد) against me concerning that.'"

(١٣) إِنَّ الْأَبْرارَ لَفِي نَعِيمٍ. (13) Indeed the pious will be in bliss. (١٤) وَإِنَّ الْفُجَّارَ لَفِي جَحِيمٍ ** (14) and indeed the vicious will be in hell. A clarification of that for which they are written down. (١٥) يَصْلَوْنَها ** (15) They shall enter it enduring its heat يَوْمَ الدِّينِ. (on the Day of Retribution.) (١٦) وَما هُمْ عَنْها بِغائِبِينَ ** (16) and they will not be absent from it. due to their eternity therein. And it was said that its meaning is that they will not be absent from it before that, since they find its scorching winds (سُمُومِهَا) in the graves. (١٧) وَما أَدْراكَ ما يَوْمُ الدِّينِ. (17) What will convey to you what the Day of Retribution is? (١٨) ثُمَّ ما أَدْراكَ ما يَوْمُ الدِّينِ ** (18) Again, what will convey to you what the Day of Retribution is? An expression of wonder and magnification of the importance of the Day, meaning: the essence of its matter is such that the knowledge (دِرَايَة) of any knower (دَارٍ) cannot comprehend it. (١٩) يَوْمَ لا تَمْلِكُ نَفْسٌ لِنَفْسٍ شَيْئاً وَالْأَمْرُ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِلَّهِ ** (19) The day when no soul will be of any avail to another soul, and the command that day will belong to Allah. alone, confirming the severity of its terror (هَوْل) and the magnificence of its affair.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When the Day of Resurrection comes, the rulers (الحُكَّام) will perish, and no ruler will remain except Allah. And the word يَوْمُ was recited with the raf' (الرَّفْع) [nominative case].

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Whoever recites these two Surahs and places them before his eyes in the obligatory (الفَرِيضَة) and supererogatory (النَّافِلَة) prayers—إِذَا السَّماءُ انْفَطَرَتْ ** (When the sky is split open) and إِذَا السَّماءُ انْشَقَّتْ ** (When the sky is split apart)—Allah will not withhold Him from any need, nor will any barrier hold him back from Allah, and Allah will continuously look upon him until He finishes the reckoning of the people.
